creator: architecture-decision-record

evidence.text: Some teams much prefer the name "decisions" over the abbreviation "ADRs". When some teams use the directory name "decisions", then it's as if a light bulb turns on, and the team starts putting more information into the directory, such as vendor decisions, planning decisions, scheduling decisions, etc. All of these kinds of information can use the same template. We hypothesize that people learn faster with words ("decisions") over abbreviations ("ADRs"), and people are more motivated to write work-in-progress docs when the word "record" is removed, and also some developers and some managers dislike the word "architecture".

provenance: GitHub API metadata and bounded README evidence for architecture-decision-record/architecture-decision-record.
relevance_evidence: The repository documents durable organizational decision memory; this is an organizational operating mechanism, not generic agent-memory research.
